changes.before: checking bodies, marking spoor, making sure the sweep was a sweep and not an invitation
the kind men always chose when they wanted a line to hold
changes.after: a line men would try to hold

changes.before: The last troll was down. The horses were warm under them and the light through the leaves had that deep amber cast
changes.after: The last troll was down, and the light through the leaves had that deep amber cast

changes.before: This morning he judged the road correctly.
Claims something's wrong in the leylines

A human mage talking about leylines made it worse in a different direction. Politics first, perhaps. Kirin Tor arrogance. A test of the stones. A spy with better vocabulary. Or someone foolish enough to touch a defense grid she did not understand
changes.after: One talking about leylines was worse. Politics first, perhaps. Kirin Tor arrogance. A test of the stones. Or someone foolish enough to touch a ward-net she did not understand

changes.before: Convocation for him. Pass for her. One of them to manage the chamber, one to put hands on the actual problem.
changes.after: Convocation for him. Pass for her.

changes.before: Understood, as always. The rest of it had been settled years ago.
leaving a riderless gap beside her that made the road feel larger and less patient
changes.after: leaving a riderless gap beside her

changes.before: let silence do most of the work. Another good choice.
not to speculate, but to sort boxes

changes.after: It smelled of theory and papers,

changes.before: with the particular stiffness of people who had been on alert too long to waste any motion
changes.after: stiff with too many hours on alert

changes.before: Cyndia took that with the same face she took everything with: no display, no hunger for more.
the ley network

changes.after: Wrong in an elven holding room.

changes.before: The system had worked.
The holding space was small and built for function
changes.after: The holding space was small and plain

changes.before: gone dusty at the hem, leather darkened at the knees from field use, and boots worn pale across the toes from too many miles in the stirrups and too much time crouched on stone
changes.after: dusty at the hem, leather dark at the knees from field use, and boots worn pale across the toes from too many miles in the stirrups

changes.before: Not a random trespasser. ... Young, yes.

observations.note: Canon check confirmed Jaina as Antonidas's apprentice and a live Kirin Tor asset; no correction needed to the identity reveal.
Canon check confirmed Sylvanas as Ranger-General and Farstrider commander; her authority framing in the outpost scenes stays accurate.
Eversong/Silvermoon/Farstrider/Amani geography all matched Warcraft lore closely enough for this AU opener.
The generic 'Pass' naming reads safely; no canon detail forced a more specific route label on-page.
No canon inaccuracies surfaced in gear, rank, or border-post behavior, so all fixes stayed stylistic rather than factual.
Trimmed patrol-cleanup prose at line 27; function stayed intact once the explanatory tail was removed.
Compressed the road-holding description at line 29; same tactical meaning, less authorial distance.
Collapsed duplicated 'good morning' sentiment at line 45; the mood survives on fewer beats.
Removed relationship footnotes at lines 57, 109, 123, and 131; Lor'themar/Sylvanas reads cleanly through action and dialogue.
Tightened the Jaina first-look paragraph at line 235 without stripping ink, frost-burn, calluses, or road wear.
Killed four document-voice phrases: 'defense grid,' 'ley network,' 'system had worked,' and 'built for function.'
Reduced vague 'something' usage from four to two; the remaining instances are tied to incomplete perception, not lazy emphasis.
Scanned for academic/clinical register contamination across narration; no remaining offenders from the zero-tolerance list survived.
Checked negation chains in lines 71, 135, 205, and 249; only line 205 read as disposable scaffolding and got cut.
Checked filter words across the whole chapter; line 71's 'heard/saw' pair stays because the beat is literally a detection pivot.
Scanned all 62 quoted lines; only one dialogue-register offender survived first pass and it was fixed at line 195.
Lor'themar's court sarcasm stayed intact; high register there reads character-specific, not AI-document voice.
The runner's report stayed clipped and field-practical after trimming 'something's wrong' to 'the leylines are wrong.'
Cyndia's speech remained plain-command register; no bureaucratic or clinical phrasing needed stripping after the leyline fix.
Sylvanas's spoken lines stayed in command shorthand: orders, questions, and finished judgments with no softening.
The chapter read cleaner than a normal v1, so I re-read the densest blocks (lines 141-155, 205-235, 241-257) word by word before approving a light pass.
The opening kill and the patrol-ease section are load-bearing contrast; pushing to a true 20% cut would have damaged the chapter engine.
The prisoner-reveal block needed compression, not flattening; cutting the physical evidence list would have hurt the chapter hook.
Continuity check passed: Jaina still has no on-page dialogue before the final line, and Sylvanas never softens her internal framing.
No expander-worthy underwritten emotional beat surfaced; this chapter is meant to end on controlled intensity, not expansion.

flags: Line 71: 'heard' and 'saw' kept — the alarm beat depends on sensory detection landing in sequence.
Line 135: 'something leaner' kept — reads as atmospheric contrast, not vague intensifier failure.
Line 187: 'answering something under it' kept — Cyndia is describing partial perception, not report prose.
Line 255: frost-on-a-blade simile kept — fresh image, sharp Sylvanas register.
